results.reason: The root cause directly addresses motor overtemperature due to cooling system blockage, matching the expected topic. The diagnosis cites specific relevant sources (conveyor_motor.md, past work orders) and uses plausible part numbers without inventing fake components.

The root cause matches the expected topic of seized bearing, and the diagnosis cites relevant sources from conveyor_motor.md and a past repair record R-0977, with realistic parts and steps, no invented details.

Root cause matches expected topic (film feed jam/roller nip/encoder) and repair steps are relevant. Sources include relevant packaging_unit.md and R-1105; the inclusion of centrifugal_pump.md is unrelated but does not invalidate grounding as no fake parts are invented.

The root cause matches the expected topic (heater cartridge/thermocouple). The diagnosis cites relevant sources (packaging_unit.md) for the fault code and corrective procedure, and does not invent any fake parts or codes.

The root cause correctly identifies recurring lint buildup as the overtemp cause, matching the expected topic of recurring overtemp/lint/prior fix reuse. The diagnosis cites relevant sources and lists plausible parts without inventing fake components or codes.
Root cause matches expected topic exactly and all repair steps/parts are supported by cited sources.

The root cause directly addresses compressor overheating due to heat rejection issues (condenser fouling/high return temp), matching the expected topic. All repair steps and parts are specific, non-fabricated, and supported by cited sources (OEM manual and past repairs). No fake codes or parts.

Root cause matches expected topic of bearing wear and high vibration, with specific bearing fault frequencies and ISO standard. Diagnosis cites relevant sources (centrifugal_pump.md, past repair R-2003) and uses real part BRG-6205. No invented parts or codes; all repair steps are plausible and reference appropriate procedures.

The diagnosis correctly identifies cavitation and impeller wear as the root cause, matching the expected topic. It cites relevant sources such as centrifugal_pump.md and past repairs, and provides plausible part numbers and repair steps without inventing fake codes or information.

The root cause directly addresses the expected topic of dirty filter/high differential pressure, and the diagnosis uses plausible part numbers and cites relevant sources without inventing any fake components or codes.
